root_text.an10.24:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā mahācundo cetīsu viharati sahajātiyaṁ.
root_text.an10.24:1.2: Tatra kho āyasmā mahācundo bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.an10.24:1.4: “Āvuso”ti kho te bhikkhū āyasmato mahācundassa paccassosuṁ.
root_text.an10.24:2.3: Tañce, āvuso, bhikkhuṁ lobho abhibhuyya tiṭṭhati,
root_text.an10.24:2.12: pāpikā icchā abhibhuyya tiṭṭhati, so evamassa veditabbo:
root_text.an10.24:2.13: ‘nāyamāyasmā tathā pajānāti yathā pajānato lobho na hoti, tathāhimaṁ āyasmantaṁ lobho abhibhuyya tiṭṭhati;
root_text.an10.24:2.14: nāyamāyasmā tathā pajānāti yathā pajānato doso na hoti …
root_text.an10.24:2.22: pāpikā icchā na hoti, tathāhimaṁ āyasmantaṁ pāpikā icchā abhibhuyya tiṭṭhatī’ti.
root_text.an10.24:3.3: Tañce, āvuso, bhikkhuṁ lobho abhibhuyya tiṭṭhati,
root_text.an10.24:3.12: pāpikā icchā abhibhuyya tiṭṭhati, so evamassa veditabbo:
root_text.an10.24:3.13: ‘nāyamāyasmā tathā pajānāti yathā pajānato lobho na hoti, tathāhimaṁ āyasmantaṁ lobho abhibhuyya tiṭṭhati;
root_text.an10.24:3.14: nāyamāyasmā tathā pajānāti yathā pajānato doso na hoti …
root_text.an10.24:3.22: pāpikā icchā na hoti, tathāhimaṁ āyasmantaṁ pāpikā icchā abhibhuyya tiṭṭhatī’ti.
root_text.an10.24:4.2: ‘jānāmimaṁ dhammaṁ, passāmimaṁ dhammaṁ, bhāvitakāyomhi bhāvitasīlo bhāvitacitto bhāvitapañño’ti.
root_text.an10.24:4.3: Tañce, āvuso, bhikkhuṁ lobho abhibhuyya tiṭṭhati,
root_text.an10.24:4.12: pāpikā icchā abhibhuyya tiṭṭhati, so evamassa veditabbo:
root_text.an10.24:4.13: ‘nāyamāyasmā tathā pajānāti yathā pajānato lobho na hoti, tathāhimaṁ āyasmantaṁ lobho abhibhuyya tiṭṭhati;
root_text.an10.24:4.14: nāyamāyasmā tathā pajānāti yathā pajānato doso na hoti …
root_text.an10.24:4.22: pāpikā icchā na hoti, tathāhimaṁ āyasmantaṁ pāpikā icchā abhibhuyya tiṭṭhatī’ti.
root_text.an10.24:5.1: Seyyathāpi, āvuso, puriso daliddova samāno aḍḍhavādaṁ vadeyya, adhanova samāno dhanavāvādaṁ vadeyya, abhogova samāno bhogavāvādaṁ vadeyya.
root_text.an10.24:5.2: So kismiñcideva dhanakaraṇīye samuppanne na sakkuṇeyya upanīhātuṁ dhanaṁ vā dhaññaṁ vā rajataṁ vā jātarūpaṁ vā.
root_text.an10.24:5.4: ‘daliddova ayamāyasmā samāno aḍḍhavādaṁ vadeti, adhanova ayamāyasmā samāno dhanavāvādaṁ vadeti, abhogavāva ayamāyasmā samāno bhogavāvādaṁ vadeti.
root_text.an10.24:5.6: Tathā hi ayamāyasmā kismiñcideva dhanakaraṇīye samuppanne na sakkoti upanīhātuṁ dhanaṁ vā dhaññaṁ vā rajataṁ vā jātarūpaṁ vā’ti.
root_text.an10.24:6.1: Evamevaṁ kho, āvuso, ñāṇavādañca bhikkhu vadamāno bhāvanāvādañca:
root_text.an10.24:6.2: ‘jānāmimaṁ dhammaṁ, passāmimaṁ dhammaṁ, bhāvitakāyomhi bhāvitasīlo bhāvitacitto bhāvitapañño’ti.
root_text.an10.24:6.3: Tañce, āvuso, bhikkhuṁ lobho abhibhuyya tiṭṭhati,
root_text.an10.24:6.12: pāpikā icchā abhibhuyya tiṭṭhati, so evamassa veditabbo:
root_text.an10.24:6.13: ‘nāyamāyasmā tathā pajānāti yathā pajānato lobho na hoti, tathāhimaṁ āyasmantaṁ lobho abhibhuyya tiṭṭhati;
root_text.an10.24:6.14: nāyamāyasmā tathā pajānāti yathā pajānato doso na hoti …
root_text.an10.24:6.22: pāpikā icchā na hoti, tathāhimaṁ āyasmantaṁ pāpikā icchā abhibhuyya tiṭṭhatī’ti.
root_text.an10.24:7.3: Tañce, āvuso, bhikkhuṁ lobho nābhibhuyya tiṭṭhati,
root_text.an10.24:7.12: pāpikā icchā nābhibhuyya tiṭṭhati, so evamassa veditabbo:
root_text.an10.24:7.13: ‘ayamāyasmā tathā pajānāti yathā pajānato lobho na hoti, tathāhimaṁ āyasmantaṁ lobho nābhibhuyya tiṭṭhati;
root_text.an10.24:7.14: tathā ayamāyasmā pajānāti yathā pajānato doso na hoti …
root_text.an10.24:7.22: pāpikā icchā na hoti, tathāhimaṁ āyasmantaṁ pāpikā icchā nābhibhuyya tiṭṭhatī’ti.
root_text.an10.24:8.3: Tañce, āvuso, bhikkhuṁ lobho nābhibhuyya tiṭṭhati,
root_text.an10.24:8.12: pāpikā icchā nābhibhuyya tiṭṭhati, so evamassa veditabbo:
root_text.an10.24:8.13: ‘tathā ayamāyasmā pajānāti yathā pajānato lobho na hoti, tathāhimaṁ āyasmantaṁ lobho nābhibhuyya tiṭṭhati;
root_text.an10.24:8.14: tathā ayamāyasmā pajānāti yathā pajānato doso na hoti …
root_text.an10.24:8.22: pāpikā icchā na hoti, tathāhimaṁ āyasmantaṁ pāpikā icchā nābhibhuyya tiṭṭhatī’ti.
root_text.an10.24:9.2: ‘jānāmimaṁ dhammaṁ, passāmimaṁ dhammaṁ, bhāvitakāyomhi bhāvitasīlo bhāvitacitto bhāvitapañño’ti.
root_text.an10.24:9.3: Tañce, āvuso, bhikkhuṁ lobho nābhibhuyya tiṭṭhati,
root_text.an10.24:9.12: pāpikā icchā nābhibhuyya tiṭṭhati, so evamassa veditabbo:
root_text.an10.24:9.13: ‘tathā ayamāyasmā pajānāti yathā pajānato lobho na hoti, tathāhimaṁ āyasmantaṁ lobho nābhibhuyya tiṭṭhati;
root_text.an10.24:9.14: tathā ayamāyasmā pajānāti yathā pajānato doso na hoti …
root_text.an10.24:9.22: pāpikā icchā na hoti, tathāhimaṁ āyasmantaṁ pāpikā icchā nābhibhuyya tiṭṭhatī’ti.
root_text.an10.24:10.1: Seyyathāpi, āvuso, puriso aḍḍhova samāno aḍḍhavādaṁ vadeyya, dhanavāva samāno dhanavāvādaṁ vadeyya, bhogavāva samāno bhogavāvādaṁ vadeyya.
root_text.an10.24:10.2: So kismiñcideva dhanakaraṇīye samuppanne sakkuṇeyya upanīhātuṁ dhanaṁ vā dhaññaṁ vā rajataṁ vā jātarūpaṁ vā.
root_text.an10.24:10.4: ‘aḍḍhova ayamāyasmā samāno aḍḍhavādaṁ vadeti, dhanavāva ayamāyasmā samāno dhanavāvādaṁ vadeti, bhogavāva ayamāyasmā samāno bhogavāvādaṁ vadeti.
root_text.an10.24:10.6: Tathā hi ayamāyasmā kismiñcideva dhanakaraṇīye samuppanne sakkoti upanīhātuṁ dhanaṁ vā dhaññaṁ vā rajataṁ vā jātarūpaṁ vā’ti.
root_text.an10.24:11.1: Evamevaṁ kho, āvuso, ñāṇavādañca bhikkhu vadamāno bhāvanāvādañca:
root_text.an10.24:11.2: ‘jānāmimaṁ dhammaṁ, passāmimaṁ dhammaṁ, bhāvitakāyomhi bhāvitasīlo bhāvitacitto bhāvitapañño’ti.
root_text.an10.24:11.3: Tañce, āvuso, bhikkhuṁ lobho nābhibhuyya tiṭṭhati,
root_text.an10.24:11.12: pāpikā icchā nābhibhuyya tiṭṭhati, so evamassa veditabbo:
root_text.an10.24:11.13: ‘tathā ayamāyasmā pajānāti yathā pajānato lobho na hoti, tathāhimaṁ āyasmantaṁ lobho nābhibhuyya tiṭṭhati;
root_text.an10.24:11.14: tathā ayamāyasmā pajānāti yathā pajānato doso na hoti …
root_text.an10.24:11.22: pāpikā icchā na hoti, tathāhimaṁ āyasmantaṁ pāpikā icchā nābhibhuyya tiṭṭhatī’”ti.

translation_text.an10.24:1.1: At one time Venerable Mahācunda was staying in the land of the Cetīs at Sahajāti.
translation_text.an10.24:2.1: “Reverends, a mendicant who makes a declaration of knowledge says:
translation_text.an10.24:2.2: ‘I know this teaching, I see this teaching.’
translation_text.an10.24:2.3: Suppose that greed masters that mendicant and keeps going.
translation_text.an10.24:2.12: or corrupt wishes master that mendicant and keep going. You should know of them:
translation_text.an10.24:2.13: ‘This venerable does not have the understanding that would eliminate greed, so greed masters them and keeps going.
translation_text.an10.24:2.14: They don’t have the understanding that would eliminate hate …
translation_text.an10.24:2.22: or corrupt wishes, so corrupt wishes master them and keep going.’
translation_text.an10.24:3.1: A mendicant who makes a declaration of development says:
translation_text.an10.24:3.2: ‘I am developed in physical endurance, ethics, mind, and wisdom.’
translation_text.an10.24:3.3: Suppose that greed masters that mendicant and keeps going.
translation_text.an10.24:3.12: or corrupt wishes master that mendicant and keep going. You should know of them:
translation_text.an10.24:3.13: ‘This venerable does not have the understanding that would eliminate greed, so greed masters them and keeps going.
translation_text.an10.24:3.14: They don’t have the understanding that would eliminate hate …
translation_text.an10.24:3.22: or corrupt wishes, so corrupt wishes master them and keep going.’
translation_text.an10.24:4.1: A mendicant who makes a declaration of both knowledge and development says:
translation_text.an10.24:4.2: ‘I know this teaching, I see this teaching. And I am developed in physical endurance, ethics, mind, and wisdom.’
translation_text.an10.24:4.3: Suppose that greed masters that mendicant and keeps going.
translation_text.an10.24:4.12: or corrupt wishes master that mendicant and keep going. You should know of them:
translation_text.an10.24:4.13: ‘This venerable does not have the understanding that would eliminate greed, so greed masters them and keeps going.
translation_text.an10.24:4.14: They don’t have the understanding that would eliminate hate …
translation_text.an10.24:4.22: or corrupt wishes, so corrupt wishes master them and keep going.’
translation_text.an10.24:5.1: Suppose a poor, needy, and penniless person was to declare themselves to be rich, affluent, and wealthy.
translation_text.an10.24:5.2: But when it came time to make a payment they weren’t able to come up with any money, grain, silver, or gold.
translation_text.an10.24:5.4: ‘This person declares themselves to be rich, affluent, and wealthy, but they are in fact poor, penniless, and needy.’
translation_text.an10.24:5.6: Because when it came time to make a payment they weren’t able to come up with any money, grain, silver, or gold.
translation_text.an10.24:6.1: In the same way, a mendicant who makes a declaration of knowledge and development says:
translation_text.an10.24:6.2: ‘I know this teaching, I see this teaching. And I am developed in physical endurance, ethics, mind, and wisdom.’
translation_text.an10.24:6.3: Suppose that greed masters that mendicant and keeps going.
translation_text.an10.24:6.12: or corrupt wishes master that mendicant and keep going. You should know of them:
translation_text.an10.24:6.13: ‘This venerable does not have the understanding that would eliminate greed, so greed masters them and keeps going.
translation_text.an10.24:6.14: They don’t have the understanding that would eliminate hate …
translation_text.an10.24:6.22: or corrupt wishes, so corrupt wishes master them and keep going.’
translation_text.an10.24:7.1: A mendicant who makes a declaration of knowledge says:
translation_text.an10.24:7.2: ‘I know this teaching, I see this teaching.’
translation_text.an10.24:7.3: Suppose that greed does not master that mendicant and keep going.
translation_text.an10.24:7.12: or corrupt wishes don’t master that mendicant and keep going. You should know of them:
translation_text.an10.24:7.13: ‘This venerable has the understanding that eliminates greed, so greed doesn’t master them and keep going.
translation_text.an10.24:7.14: They have the understanding that eliminates hate …
translation_text.an10.24:7.22: and corrupt wishes, so corrupt wishes don’t master them and keep going.’
translation_text.an10.24:8.1: A mendicant who makes a declaration of development says:
translation_text.an10.24:8.2: ‘I am developed in physical endurance, ethics, mind, and wisdom.’
translation_text.an10.24:8.3: Suppose that greed does not master that mendicant and keep going.
translation_text.an10.24:8.12: or corrupt wishes don’t master that mendicant and keep going. You should know of them:
translation_text.an10.24:8.13: ‘This venerable has the understanding that eliminates greed, so greed doesn’t master them and keep going.
translation_text.an10.24:8.14: They have the understanding that eliminates hate …
translation_text.an10.24:8.22: and corrupt wishes, so corrupt wishes don’t master them and keep going.’
translation_text.an10.24:9.1: A mendicant who makes a declaration of both knowledge and development says:
translation_text.an10.24:9.2: ‘I know this teaching, I see this teaching. And I am developed in physical endurance, ethics, mind, and wisdom.’
translation_text.an10.24:9.3: Suppose that greed does not master that mendicant and keep going.
translation_text.an10.24:9.12: or corrupt wishes don’t master that mendicant and keep going. You should know of them:
translation_text.an10.24:9.13: ‘This venerable has the understanding that eliminates greed, so greed doesn’t master them and keep going.
translation_text.an10.24:9.14: They have the understanding that eliminates hate …
translation_text.an10.24:9.22: and corrupt wishes, so corrupt wishes don’t master them and keep going.’
translation_text.an10.24:10.1: Suppose a rich, affluent, and wealthy person was to declare themselves to be rich, affluent, and wealthy.
translation_text.an10.24:10.2: And whenever it came time to make a payment they were able to come up with the money, grain, silver, or gold.
translation_text.an10.24:10.4: ‘This person declares themselves to be rich, affluent, and wealthy, and they are in fact rich, affluent, and wealthy.’
translation_text.an10.24:10.6: Because when it came time to make a payment they were able to come up with the money, grain, silver, or gold.
translation_text.an10.24:11.1: In the same way, a mendicant who makes a declaration of knowledge and development says:
translation_text.an10.24:11.2: ‘I know this teaching, I see this teaching. And I am developed in physical endurance, ethics, mind, and wisdom.’
translation_text.an10.24:11.3: Suppose that greed does not master that mendicant and keep going.
translation_text.an10.24:11.12: or corrupt wishes don’t master that mendicant and keep going. You should know of them:
translation_text.an10.24:11.13: ‘This venerable has the understanding that eliminates greed, so greed doesn’t master them and keep going.
translation_text.an10.24:11.14: They have the understanding that eliminates hate …
translation_text.an10.24:11.22: and corrupt wishes, so corrupt wishes don’t master them and keep going.’”
